perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information. (a) Each of Stockholders severally agrees that such Stockholder shall not, at any time following the effectiveness of the Merger, print, publish, divulge or communicate to any person, firm, corporation or other business organization or use for his or their own account any business contacts, customers, suppliers, technology, product designs and specifications, know-how, trade secrets, marketing techniques, promotional materials or other secret or confidential information relating to the Business or any other businesses then being engaged in by AUGI or any of its subsidiaries, or any secret or confidential information relating to the affairs, dealings and concerns of the Company, AUGI or any of the subsidiaries of AUGI (collectively, the "Confidential Information") which the Stockholder has received or obtained while an employee or member of the Board of Directors of AUGI or the Company (whether or not developed, devised, or otherwise created in whole or in part by the efforts of the Stockholder). The term "Confidential Information" does not include information which information and its relevance in the particular instance (A) is a matter of common knowledge or public record, (B) is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of disclosure by the Stockholder, (C) which information and its relevance is generally known in Business, (D) the Stockholder can demonstrate that such information and its relevance in the applicable instance, was already known to the recipient thereof other than by reason of any breach of any obligation of the Stockholder under this Agreement or any other confidentiality or non-disclosure agreement, or (E) the information is required to be disclosed by law or pursuant to court order or subpoeona.
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential Information. (a) Each of Stockholders severally agrees that such Stockholder shall not, at any time following the effectiveness of the Merger, print, publish, divulge or communicate to any person, firm, corporation or other business organization or use for his or their own account any business contacts, customers, suppliers, technology, product designs and specifications, know-how, trade secrets, marketing techniques, promotional materials or other secret or confidential information relating to the Business or any other businesses then being engaged in by AUGI or any of its subsidiaries, or any secret or confidential information relating to the affairs, dealings and concerns of the Company, AUGI or any of the subsidiaries of AUGI (collectively, the "Confidential Information") which the Stockholder has received or obtained while an employee or member of the Board of Directors of AUGI or the Company (whether or not developed, devised, or otherwise created in whole or in part by the efforts of the Stockholder).
perturbation.explanation: By removing the exceptions (A) through (E) to the definition of 'Confidential Information,' the contract now broadly prohibits the Stockholders from disclosing or using any information related to the Business, AUGI, or the Company, regardless of whether that information is publicly available or required to be disclosed by law. This omission creates a contradiction because it is virtually impossible for the Stockholders to completely avoid using or referencing publicly available information, potentially putting them in breach of the agreement even for innocuous acts. This also contradicts any legal obligation to disclose information under subpoena.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidential Information. (a) Each of Stockholders severally agrees that such Stockholder shall not, at any time following the effectiveness of the Merger, print, publish, divulge or communicate to any person, firm, corporation or other business organization or use for his or their own account any business contacts, customers, suppliers, technology, product designs and specifications, know-how, trade secrets, marketing techniques, promotional materials or other secret or confidential information relating to the Business or any other businesses then being engaged in by AUGI or any of its subsidiaries, or any secret or confidential information relating to the affairs, dealings and concerns of the Company, AUGI or any of the subsidiaries of AUGI (collectively, the "Confidential Information") which the Stockholder has received or obtained while an employee or member of the Board of Directors of AUGI or the Company (whether or not developed, devised, or otherwise created in whole or in part by the efforts of the Stockholder).
perturbation.justification: The original text defined 'Confidential Information' with several exceptions (A-E). The changed text omits these exceptions, making the definition of 'Confidential Information' much broader. The contradicted text, which is identical to the original text, operates under the assumption that these exceptions exist. By removing the exceptions, the changed text creates a direct conflict, as it now prohibits the Stockholders from disclosing information that would have been permissible under the original agreement, therefore, a strong contradiction exists. The Perturbation Type 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' and explanation clearly supports that essential language was removed creating a contradiction with other sections.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding anything to the contrary, express or implied, contained in this Agreement, the Merger Agreement or in any other "Transaction Document" (as that term is defined in the Merger Agreement), no future act of omission or commission by AUGI or any of its affiliates, or other actual or alleged breach by AUGI or such affiliates of any of their covenants or agreements contained in any Transaction Documents (in addition to this Agreement), shall in any way be deemed to modify, limit or terminate the covenants and agreements of the Stockholders contained in Section 1 and Section 2 hereof, all of which shall remain in full force and effect in accordance with their respective terms.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding anything to the contrary, express or implied, contained in this Agreement, the Merger Agreement or in any other "Transaction Document" (as that term is defined in the Merger Agreement), no future act of omission or commission by AUGI or any of its affiliates, or other actual or alleged breach by AUGI or such affiliates of any of their covenants or agreements contained in any Transaction Documents (in addition to this Agreement), shall in any way be deemed to modify, limit or terminate the covenants and agreements of the Stockholders contained in Section 1 hereof, which shall remain in full force and effect in accordance with their respective terms.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'and Section 2 hereof' from this clause, the agreement no longer explicitly protects the confidentiality obligations in Section 2 from being modified, limited, or terminated due to AUGI's actions. This creates an in-text contradiction. While Section 2 outlines the confidentiality obligations, Section 3(b) now only guarantees the non-compete agreement in Section 1. This could imply that a breach by AUGI could release the Stockholders from their confidentiality obligations but not their non-compete, creating uncertainty in enforcement and contradicting the intent of a comprehensive agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidential Information. (a) Each of Stockholders severally agrees that such Stockholder shall not, at any time following the effectiveness of the Merger, print, publish, divulge or communicate to any person, firm, corporation or other business organization or use for his or their own account any business contacts, customers, suppliers, technology, product designs and specifications, know-how, trade secrets, marketing techniques, promotional materials or other secret or confidential information relating to the Business or any other businesses then being engaged in by AUGI or any of its subsidiaries, or any secret or confidential information relating to the affairs, dealings and concerns of the Company, AUGI or any of the subsidiaries of AUGI (collectively, the "Confidential Information") which the Stockholder has received or obtained while an employee or member of the Board of Directors of AUGI or the Company (whether or not developed, devised, or otherwise created in whole or in part by the efforts of the Stockholder).
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly protected the covenants in both Section 1 and Section 2 from being modified or terminated due to actions by AUGI or its affiliates. The changed text removes 'and Section 2 hereof,' limiting this protection to only Section 1. Section 2 outlines the Stockholders' confidentiality obligations. By removing Section 2 from the protection clause, a breach by AUGI could potentially release the Stockholders from their confidentiality obligations (Section 2), while their non-compete obligations (Section 1) remain intact. This creates a contradiction with the intent of the agreement, as the redacted Section 3(b) no longer ensures that Section 2 remains in full force and effect regardless of AUGI's actions, contradicting the standalone confidentiality requirements outlined in the Contradicted Text.

perturbation.original_text: Without intending to limit the remedies available to AUGI or the Company, each Stockholder further agrees that damages at law will be an insufficient remedy to AUGI if such Stockholder(s) breaches the terms of this Agreement and that AUGI or the Company may apply for and have injunctive relief in any court of competent jurisdiction to restrain the breach or threatened breach of or otherwise to specifically enforce any of the covenants contained herein, in each case without the necessity of proving damages or posting bond.
perturbation.changed_text: Without intending to limit the remedies available to AUGI or the Company, each Stockholder further agrees that damages at law will be an insufficient remedy to AUGI if such Stockholder(s) breaches the terms of this Agreement and that AUGI or the Company may apply for and have injunctive relief in any court of competent jurisdiction to restrain the breach or threatened breach of the covenants contained in Section 1, in each case without the necessity of proving damages or posting bond.
perturbation.explanation: By restricting the injunctive relief clause to only Section 1 (Non-Competition), AUGI's ability to enforce Section 2 (Confidentiality) through injunctive relief is now ambiguous. While damages are stated as insufficient, the specific mention of injunctive relief for Section 1 only implies that it may not be available for breaches of Section 2. This creates an in-text contradiction as Section 2 still defines confidentiality obligations, implying enforceability, but this modification raises doubts about the availability of a key legal remedy, contradicting its effectiveness.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidential Information. (a) Each of Stockholders severally agrees that such Stockholder shall not, at any time following the effectiveness of the Merger, print, publish, divulge or communicate to any person, firm, corporation or other business organization or use for his or their own account any business contacts, customers, suppliers, technology, product designs and specifications, know-how, trade secrets, marketing techniques, promotional materials or other secret or confidential information relating to the Business or any other businesses then being engaged in by AUGI or any of its subsidiaries, or any secret or confidential information relating to the affairs, dealings and concerns of the Company, AUGI or any of the subsidiaries of AUGI (collectively, the "Confidential Information") which the Stockholder has received or obtained while an employee or member of the Board of Directors of AUGI or the Company (whether or not developed, devised, or otherwise created in whole or in part by the efforts of the Stockholder).
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original text by restricting the application of injunctive relief specifically to breaches of covenants in Section 1 (Non-Competition). The original text allowed for injunctive relief for any breach of the agreement. The contradicted text, Section 2 (Confidentiality), outlines confidentiality obligations. By limiting injunctive relief to Section 1, the changed text creates a contradiction. While Section 2 implies enforceability of confidentiality obligations, the limited injunctive relief clause makes it ambiguous whether AUGI can seek immediate court intervention (injunctive relief) for a breach of confidentiality, contradicting the original broad enforcement capability. This aligns with the Omissions - In Text Contradiction definition, specifically the scenario where essential language is removed or altered in one section, creating a contradiction with obligations stated in other sections.

perturbation.original_text: Any paragraph, sentence, phrase or other provision of this Agreement which is in conflict with any applicable statute, rule or other law shall be deemed, if possible, to be modified or altered to conform thereto or, if not possible, to be omitted herefrom. The invalidity of any portion hereof shall not affect the force and effect of the remaining valid portion hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: The invalidity of any portion hereof shall not affect the force and effect of the remaining valid portion hereof.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause regarding conflicting statutes, rules, or laws, the agreement no longer explicitly prioritizes compliance with applicable laws. This creates an in-text contradiction if a section of the agreement, as modified by previous perturbations, now violates a law. The agreement, as a whole, implies it intends to be legal and enforceable; however, the absence of this clause makes it possible for an illegal provision to stand, contradicting the presumed intent of lawful agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The invalidity of any portion hereof shall not affect the force and effect of the remaining valid portion hereof.
perturbation.justification: The original text included a clause prioritizing compliance with applicable statutes, rules, or other laws. This was removed in the changed text. The contradicted text states that 'The invalidity of any portion hereof shall not affect the force and effect of the remaining valid portion hereof.' Without the original clause regarding conflicting statutes, the agreement no longer explicitly prioritizes compliance with laws. Therefore, the change creates a strong in-text contradiction because a now illegal provision can stand, contradicting the intent of a lawful agreement and the force and effect of the valid portions.
